methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante is a Series E climate and energy company founded by Claude Letourneau that has raised $318M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 private peers (including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape) and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though peers at identical stage and valuation tier are sparse within the Climate & Energy sector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($318M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.4x multiplier for Series E advancement yields $445M. This method is suitable given Svante's late-stage position, though peer-median analysis could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or narrow-tier comparables in the sector—only 2 adjacent-stage peers met basic filters, below the 3-point minimum threshold for robust median derivation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $378M to $512M, with a median point of $445M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.4x step-up multiplier for Svante's specific market conditions and the absence of tighter peer clustering at Series E in the climate tech space. Given the single-method foundation, sensitivity to both the multiplier assumption and late-stage market conditions in energy technology represents material range risk.
